name: Multi-Grain Soybean Bread (Conventional Recipe)
recipeInstructions:
1. Combine warm water, soybean oil, honey, and dry yeast in a measuring cup.
2. Allow to stand for 5 minutes.
3. Measure flours and other ingredients into a mixing bowl. Blend the warm liquids into the flour mixture. Beat with an electric mixer at medium speed for two minutes, occasionally scraping the sides of the bowl. The dough will be stiff.
4. Turn out onto a lightly floured board and knead the mixture until it becomes smooth and elastic, and the dough does not stick to the board. Place the dough in a bowl that has been rubbed down with soy oil, and allow to double in volume.
5. Punch down. Knead lightly and shape into a loaf. Place in a greased 9-inch x 4-1/2 inches loaf pan and let rise to double volume, approximately 30 minutes.
6. Bake in a 350°F oven for 35 to 40 minutes. Allow to cool on a rack.
Yield: one loaf of delicious and nutritious bread.
